headline: Driverless PostBus rams bank façade in Nunningen, SO.
summary: On the evening of May 15, an autonomous PostBus vehicle crossed a traffic island and crashed into the front of a bank branch in Nunningen (canton Solothurn); building and bus both sustained significant damage.

headline: Switzerland backs Special Tribunal for Ukraine aggression.
summary: At the Council of Europe foreign ministers' meeting in Chișinău on May 15, Switzerland formally notified accession to the Enlarged Partial Agreement establishing the Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine; 37 countries supported the agreement.

headline: "No to 10 million" vote: record campaign spending ahead of June 14.
summary: The SVP's immigration-cap initiative — which would freeze the permanent resident population below 10 million before 2050 — is generating unprecedented campaign budgets: CHF 9 million from opponents (Economiesuisse alone contributing CHF 4.2 million) vs.

headline: High African Swine Fever risk flagged by FSVO.
summary: The Federal Food Safety and Veterinary Office rates the risk of ASF transmission to Switzerland as high following a renewed outbreak in northern Italy in April, the first in that country after years of declining European case counts.

headline: Switzerland joins Galileo and EGNOS under Bilaterals III space annex.
summary: Following the March 2 Bilaterals III signing, the EU Agency for the Space Programme (EUSPA) has confirmed that Swiss firms may now bid on Galileo/EGNOS procurements and Swiss nationals may apply for EUSPA positions — a significant opening for Switzerland's space-tech sector.

headline: Xi warns Trump: Taiwan mishandling risks "conflict."
summary: At a two-day Beijing summit ending May 15, Xi Jinping told Trump that Taiwan is "the most important issue in U.S.-China relations" and that mishandling it would put ties "in great jeopardy" — his sharpest public language on the topic to date during the Trump term.

headline: Israel targets Hamas military chief Haddad in Gaza City strike.
summary: On May 15, Israeli Air Force jets dropped 13 bombs on a residential building in Gaza City's Rimal neighbourhood targeting Izz al-Din al-Haddad — described by Israeli officials as the last senior Hamas leader with direct involvement in October 7 and current head of Hamas's military wing.

headline: Iran: FM says "lack of trust" stalls Hormuz talks as Trump departs Beijing.
summary: Iranian Foreign Minister told France 24 on May 15 that mutual distrust is the primary obstacle to any deal; Pakistan-mediated US-Iran negotiations remain at an impasse over whether Iran's nuclear programme must be included.

headline: Hungary: Péter Magyar sworn in, EU reset begins.
summary: Magyar was sworn in as Prime Minister on May 9, ending Orbán's 16-year rule following his Tisza party's 53.6%-to-37.8% victory on April 12 — a record 77% turnout.

headline: India-Pakistan: one year after the 2025 conflict, Indus Waters Treaty still frozen.
summary: Analysts mark the first anniversary of the May 7–10, 2025 air exchange; Pakistan's military warned this week that future confrontation could cause "cataclysmic devastation." India has not reinstated the Indus Waters Treaty (suspended April 2025), which supplies over 80% of Pakistan's agricultural wa

headline: Alzheimer's: Sox9 turns astrocytes into amyloid-clearing vacuums.
summary: Baylor College of Medicine researchers showed that overexpressing transcription factor Sox9 in mouse astrocytes upregulates phagocytic receptor MEGF10, dramatically increasing amyloid-β plaque clearance and preserving cognitive function even in animals already showing memory deficits.

headline: JWST: first direct surface-composition read of a rocky exoplanet.
summary: Mid-infrared spectroscopy (5–12 μm) of tidally-locked super-Earth LHS 3844 b reveals a dark, basalt-or-olivine-rich surface with no detectable atmosphere — the first time JWST has directly characterised an exoplanet's surface mineralogy rather than its atmosphere.

headline: Cambrian Lagerstätte rewrites deep-sea early-life timeline.
summary: A new fossil site yields exceptionally preserved deep-marine Cambrian specimens — the most biodiverse window yet into that poorly-understood environment — including putative early deuterostome relatives that push back vertebrate lineage origins.

headline: Bird eye pushed to vertebrate sensory limits.
summary: New findings reported in *Quanta Magazine* (13 May) show avian retinas — which pack four or five photoreceptor types into extraordinarily dense mosaics — have been driven by evolutionary pressure to the physical boundaries of vertebrate visual architecture; the work illuminates sensory diversity acr

headline: [2605.10181] A Comparative Study of Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Out-of-Distribution Detection.
summary: Traditional ML classifiers match deep-learning OOD detectors on accuracy while delivering substantially lower inference latency — a practical case for reconsidering ML-over-DL in deployment-constrained settings.

headline: [2605.05358] Balancing Stability and Plasticity in Sequentially Trained Early-Exiting Neural Networks.
summary: Early-exit networks enable adaptive computation through intermediate classifiers, but sequential training causes newly added exits to interfere with previously learned ones.

headline: [2605.00419] Rethinking LLM Ensembling from the Perspective of Mixture Models.
summary: Reframes ensemble methods as mixture models that stochastically select a single model at each generation step rather than computing a full weighted distribution — may substantially reduce ensemble inference cost while preserving output diversity.

headline: [2605.02884] Unsupervised Machine Learning for Detecting Structural Anomalies in European Regional Statistics.
summary: Applies and benchmarks Isolation Forest and Local Outlier Factor against NUTS-level EU regional data; useful methodological reference for anomaly detection in structured socioeconomic datasets.

headline: USD/CHF holding ~0.78; EUR rates unavailable.
summary: USD/CHF traded in the 0.776–0.784 range during the first two weeks of May, last recorded at ~0.784 on May 14.

headline: Asia equities softer on Xi–Trump Taiwan exchange.
summary: Hang Seng closed down ~1.6% on May 15, weighed by Xi's sharpest-yet Taiwan warning to Trump; the Nikkei 225 was trading in choppy range around 62,400 earlier in the week.

headline: US futures near flat pre-market.
summary: Dow futures -0.02% (~50,000 maintained after Cisco earnings), S&P 500 -0.02%, Nasdaq 100 +0.06%.
